summary: Re-test of Documents list-refresh bug from iter4. Logged in as agente@demo.it, navigated to /documenti, uploaded a 435-byte real PDF via the modal. Verified the new card appeared IMMEDIATELY (no reload) with correct testid, name, size, FileText icon and download button. Download triggered a real browser download with filename 'iter5_test.pdf' and 435 bytes / %PDF header (content match). Delete removed the card optimistically (gone within 150ms of click), DELETE returned 200, and after a hard reload + direct GET /api/documents the doc was no longer present. Iteration-4 HIGH-priority bug is FIXED.

ui_bugs.issue: Console warning persists: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}'. Add a <DialogDescription> (or aria-describedby) to silence Radix a11y warning. Non-blocking.

action_items: (Optional, LOW) Add DialogDescription/aria-describedby to the 'Nuovo documento' DialogContent to remove the Radix accessibility warning.
critical_code_review_comments: Documents.jsx remove(): optimistic filter is correct; rollback on failure currently calls load() — good. Consider also showing the previous toast text and re-inserting the exact removed item to preserve order without an extra GET.
Documents.jsx download(): for legacy docs without storage_path it falls back to doc.url/window.open or a toast. Acceptable but worth noting in UX.

Backend GET /api/documents/{id}/download: filename in Content-Disposition is still not RFC 5987 escaped (would break for accented filenames like 'Contratto è.pdf'). Use filename* with urllib.parse.quote.
DialogContent missing DialogDescription — minor a11y warning.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (upload-immediate-render, optimistic delete, download content match all PASS)

seed_data_creation: Uploaded one TEST_iter5_<ts>.pdf (435 B) and deleted it via the UI within the same run. No leftover test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter4 HIGH bug 'list refresh after upload/delete in /documenti' is verified FIXED via optimistic state updates in Documents.jsx (setDocs(prev => [newDoc, ...prev]) on upload; optimistic filter on delete with rollback). Only remaining minor item is the Radix DialogDescription a11y warning. Backend untouched. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://visit-planner-32.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca_of_the_issue: Iter4 root cause: UploadForm.onDone fired setOpen(false) and then a non-awaited load() — combined with React 18 batching and the Dialog unmount/re-render cycle, the refetched docs setState was being applied to a stale closure and dropped. Iter5 fix: backend response from POST /api/documents/upload is passed directly to onDone(newDoc) and Documents prepends it via setDocs(prev => [newDoc, ...prev]); delete uses optimistic filter before await api.delete(). Both behaviours now confirmed in-browser: new card appears within ~800 ms with no GET dependency, and deleted card disappears within 150 ms; backend persistence verified by full reload + direct GET /api/documents.
